    *   It has a clear border separating it from the surrounding text.
    *   The text inside includes "A British Bank conducted on British Lines.", the bank name, addresses in Geneva, Paris, and London, and a list of other cities.

    *   The page dimensions are 2479 x 3508 pixels.
    *   I need to estimate the coordinates of the advertisement box.
    *   The ad is located in the lower right section.
    *   Looking at the full image, the ad starts roughly around the middle vertically and extends to the bottom.

    *   Let's estimate coordinates relative to the full image size.
    *   Top edge (y0): It starts below the "Swiss Coal Import Tax Abolished" article. This is roughly halfway down the page. Let's say around y=1300.
    *   Left edge (x0): It starts after the left column of text. The right column text starts around x=1200. The ad is further right. Let's estimate x0 around 1200.
    *   Bottom edge (y1): It goes almost to the bottom of the page. Maybe y=2800.
    *   Right edge (x1): It ends before the right margin. Maybe x=2000.

    Let's refine the coordinates based on visual inspection.
    *   The page is split into roughly two main columns, but the right side has a mix of articles and the ad.
    *   The ad box is clearly defined by a black border.
    *   Top of the box: Just below the line separating the "Swiss Coal Import Tax Abolished" article. This looks to be around y=1300.
    *   Left of the box: Aligned with the start of the right-hand column text block. This looks to be around x=1200.
    *   Bottom of the box: Near the bottom edge of the text area. Around y=2800.
    *   Right of the box: Aligned with the right margin of the text area. Around x=2000.

    Let's look closer.
    The ad box starts with "A British Bank conducted on British Lines."
    The box ends with "Correspondents throughout the World."
    The box is clearly distinct.
    Coordinates estimation:
    x0: ~1200
    y0: ~1300
    x1: ~2000
    y1: ~2800

    Let's try to be more precise.
    The image width is 2479. The text columns seem to take up about 80% of the width. So text area is roughly 0 to 2000.
    The ad is in the right column.
    The top of the ad is below the "Swiss Coal Import Tax Abolished" section. That section ends around y=1300.
    The bottom of the ad is near the bottom of the page content. The page content ends around y=2800.
    The left edge of the ad aligns with the left edge of the right column text. That's around x=1200.
    The right edge of the ad aligns with the right edge of the right column text. That's around x=2000.

    So, [1200, 1300, 2000, 2800] seems like a reasonable estimate.
    Let's re-evaluate.
    The ad box is quite large.
    Top: ~1300
    Left: ~1200
    Bottom: ~2800
    Right: ~2000

    Wait, looking at the full image again.
    The ad is in the bottom right.
    The top of the ad is roughly 1/3 down from the bottom? No, it's in the bottom half.
    Let's look at the "Swiss Coal Import Tax Abolished" article. It ends with a horizontal line. The ad starts below that line.
    The "Swiss Coal Import Tax Abolished" article is in the right column.
    The ad is below it.
    So y0 is definitely around 1300.
    The ad extends down to the bottom of the text block.
    The text block ends around y=2800.
    The ad width is similar to the column width.
    The column starts around x=1200 and ends around x=2000.
    So the box is roughly [1200, 1300, 2000, 2800].

    Let's refine the numbers.
    x0: 1200
    y0: 1300
    x1: 2000
    y1: 2800

    Let's check the aspect ratio.
    Width: 800. Height: 1500.
    This looks like a tall rectangle, which matches the visual appearance of the ad box.

    Let's try to be more precise with pixel values.
    The image is 2479 wide.
    The ad is in the right column.
    The right column starts around x=1200.
    The right column ends around x=2000.
    The ad starts below the "Swiss Coal Import Tax Abolished" article.
    The "Swiss Coal Import Tax Abolished" article starts around y=600 and ends around y=1300.
    So y0 is around 1300.
    The ad ends near the bottom of the page content.
    The page content ends around y=2800.
    So y1 is around 2800.

    Let's adjust slightly.
    x0: 1200
    y0: 1300
    x1: 2000
    y1: 2800

    Wait, looking closer at the image, the ad box is actually quite distinct.
    The top line is "A British Bank conducted on British Lines."
    The bottom line is "Correspondents throughout the World."
    The box is clearly visible.
    Let's assume the coordinates are:
    [1200, 1300, 2000, 2800]

    Let's double check.
    The ad is for "Lloyds and National Provincial Foreign Bank Limited."
    It is the only advertisement on this page.
    The other text blocks are articles ("English and American Travellers as we see them", "Belgian Share Issue in Switzerland", "Swiss Coal Import Tax Abolished").

    So, only one advertisement.
    Box: [1200, 1300, 2000, 2800]
